2603.14541.title: Expert Mind: A Retrieval-Augmented Architecture for Expert Knowledge Preservation in the Energy Sector
2603.14541.paper_summary: The departure of subject-matter experts from industrial organizations results in the irreversible loss of tacit knowledge that is rarely captured through conventional documentation practices. This paper proposes Expert Mind, an experimental system that leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), large language models (LLMs), and multimodal capture techniques to preserve, structure, and make queryable the deep expertise of organizational knowledge holders. Drawing on the specific context of the energy sector, where decades of operational experience risk being lost to an aging workforce, we describe the system architecture, processing pipeline, ethical framework, and evaluation methodology. The proposed system addresses the knowledge elicitation problem through structured interviews, think-aloud sessions, and text corpus ingestion, which are subsequently embedded into a vector store and queried through a conversational interface. Preliminary design considerations suggest Expert Mind can significantly reduce knowledge transfer latency and improve onboarding efficiency. Ethical dimensions including informed consent, intellectual property, and the right to erasure are addressed as first-class design constraints.
2603.14541.paper_summary_zh: 主題專家的離開使工業組織面臨不可逆轉的隱性知識損失，而這種知識很少能通過傳統的文檔實踐來捕捉。本文提出了一個名為 Expert Mind 的實驗系統，該系統利用檢索增強生成（RAG）、大型語言模型（LLMs）和多模態捕捉技術，以保留、結構化並使組織知識持有者的深厚專業知識可查詢。基於能源行業的具體背景，這裡的運營經驗數十年面臨著因老齡化勞動力而可能流失的風險，我們描述了系統架構、處理管道、倫理框架和評估方法論。所提議的系統通過結構化訪談、思維大聲思考會議和文本語料庫攝取來解決知識引出問題，這些資料隨後被嵌入向量存儲中，並通過對話界面進行查詢。初步設計考量表明，Expert Mind 可以顯著減少知識轉移延遲並提高入職效率。倫理維度包括知情同意、知識產權和刪除權被作為首要設計約束來處理。
2603.14541.author: Diego Ezequiel Cervera et.al.
